test_cases.description: base64Encode encodes a known byte array to the expected base64 string
base64Encode encodes an empty Uint8Array to an empty string
base64Encode encodes a single byte correctly
base64Encode throws or errors when given a non-Uint8Array input
test_cases.input_description: A plain string 'hello' instead of Uint8Array

test_cases.description: base64Encode errors when window.base64js is unavailable
test_cases.input_description: Valid Uint8Array but window.base64js is undefined

test_cases.description: base64Decode decodes a known base64 string to the expected bytes
base64Decode decodes an empty string to an empty Uint8Array
base64Decode throws when given invalid base64 characters
base64Decode errors when window.base64js is unavailable
test_cases.input_description: Valid base64 string but window.base64js is undefined

test_cases.description: base64Decode(base64Encode(x)) produces identical bytes for any Uint8Array x
assertions: decoded bytes are identical to original input for all test vectors

test_cases.description: verifySignature returns true for a valid Ed25519 signature over a known message
test_cases.input_description: Real Ed25519 keypair signs 'Hello, World!' — base64 encoded sig and pubkey

test_cases.description: verifySignature returns true for a valid signature over a UTF-8 emoji message
test_cases.input_description: Real Ed25519 keypair signs '🔐🚀 Héllo Wörld! 日本語' — base64 encoded sig and pubkey

test_cases.description: verifySignature returns false when message has been tampered with
test_cases.input_description: Valid signature for 'Hello, World!' but message changed to 'Hello, World?'

test_cases.description: verifySignature returns false when a different public key is used
test_cases.input_description: Valid signature for 'Hello, World!' but verified against a different keypair's public key

test_cases.description: verifySignature returns false (fail-closed) for invalid base64 in signature
verifySignature returns false (fail-closed) for invalid base64 in publicKey
verifySignature returns false when decoded signature is not 64 bytes
test_cases.input_description: signature is base64 of only 32 bytes (too short)

test_cases.description: verifySignature returns false when decoded publicKey is not 32 bytes
test_cases.input_description: publicKey is base64 of only 16 bytes (too short)

test_cases.description: verifySignature returns false when decoded publicKey is 33 bytes (one too many)
verifySignature returns false for empty message string
verifySignature returns false for empty signature string
verifySignature returns false for empty publicKey string
verifySignature returns false when message is null
verifySignature returns false when message is undefined
verifySignature returns false when message is a number
verifySignature returns false when signature is null
verifySignature returns false when publicKey is undefined
verifySignature returns false when window.nacl is unavailable
test_cases.input_description: All valid string inputs but window.nacl is undefined

test_cases.description: verifySignature never throws an exception regardless of input
test_cases.input_description: Various malformed inputs: null, undefined, numbers, objects, empty strings, invalid base64
test_cases.expected_output_description: All calls return false; none throw

test_cases.description: verifySignature returns false when decoded signature is 63 bytes (one too few)
verifySignature returns false when decoded signature is 65 bytes (one too many)
verifySignature returns false when decoded publicKey is 31 bytes (one too few)
